# SDD Detection — Output Template

<!-- Generated by Phase A Step 1 — the SDD-detection subagent driven by
     `references/sdd-detection.md`. Single-line JSON written to
     `plans/upsale/sdd-detection.json`. Consumed by:
     - SKILL.md Phase A → Phase B handoff (gates business track spawn).
     - every business/01-discovery/<NN>-<slug>.md per-item subagent (resolves specsRoot for spec-file reads). -->

## File location

`plans/upsale/sdd-detection.json`

## JSON shape (REQUIRED)

```json
{
  "isSDD": true,
  "signals": [
    {"kind": "specs-dir", "path": "specs/", "weight": 3},
    {"kind": "spec-file", "path": "specs/feature-list.md", "weight": 2}
  ],
  "specsRoot": "specs/"
}
```

## Field semantics

- `isSDD` — boolean. `true` when the repo follows Spec-Driven Development (a `specsRoot` directory with ≥1 spec-style artifact). Gates the business track in SKILL.md.
- `signals` — array of evidence entries, each:
  - `kind` — short tag: `specs-dir` | `spec-file` | `feature-list` | `user-stories` | `screen-list` | `data-model` | `permissions` | `background-logic` | `system-overview` | `route-list`.
  - `path` — relative repo path that produced the signal.
  - `weight` — 1 | 2 | 3 (3 = directory presence + ≥3 spec artifacts; 2 = canonical artifact name; 1 = weak signal).
- `specsRoot` — relative path to the spec root (`specs/`, `docs/specs/`, or `.specify/`) when `isSDD == true`. Empty string `""` when `isSDD == false`.

## Fallback

When the subagent cannot determine SDD presence (filesystem error, unreadable directory), it MUST emit `{"isSDD": false, "signals": [], "specsRoot": ""}` — never raise. `references/sdd-detection.md` is the source of truth for the procedure; this template documents the resulting JSON contract for human readers.
